rulings.Case Title: ATHANASSIOS DIACAKIS VS. SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS, SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING COMMISSION DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED. Petitioner's Amended Petition failed to state facts showing any act the Respondents are compelled to perform in relation to the Respondents' March 15, 2022, decision approving MJ Mission Dolores' application to build a 5-story building. To the extent that the Amended Petition alleges that the March 15, 2022, decision prevented the Respondents from issuing the June 27, 2023, decision, the Respondents' Demurrer is sustained WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as time-barred by Government Code section 65009(c)(1)(E) and section 65009(e).=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FREDA A. LEEPER VS. PORTIA L. OSBORNE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT PORTIA OSBORNE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege whether an agreement exists between the parties such that the Partition of Real Property Act ("PRPA") does not apply. rulings.Case Title: FREDA A. LEEPER VS. PORTIA L. OSBORNE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT PORTIA OSBORNE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting the application of costs and fees under Code of Civil Procedure section 874.010 rather than under section 874.321.5. (See CCP 874.313(a).) Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice of various pieces of legislative history of PRPA is GRANTED. (Evid. Code 452.) Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice of a Westlaw form partition complaint filed in support of the Opposition is GRANTED. (Evid. Code 452(h).) Plaintiff's April 17, 2024, filing requesting the Court strike arguments in Defendant's Reply is STRICKEN. The accompanying Request for Judicial Notice of materials compiled by Defendant's counsel for a legal presentation is DENIED. Plaintiff provides no foundation for this evidence, and it is irrelevant to the matter at hand. (Evid. Code 403, 210.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: POSEE CHUNG VS. RICK QURESHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Vacate;
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT RICK QURESHI NOTICE OF MOTION; MOTION TO VACATE; is DENIED. Defendant fails to establish that the judgment is void pursuant to Civil Code of Procedure section 473(b). Further, Defendant essentially asks the Court to reconsider Judge Karnow's Order Denying Defendant's Motion for New Trial but provides no changed circumstances that would warrant such reconsideration. (CCP section 1008(b).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
